description: Natural-only over-trigger probe (no exam sibling): the intent gate must fire once for a NEW automation and stay quiet for mid-build increments. Turn 1 is a plain workflow build; turn 2 is a small edit to the same automation. Over-classification (re-loading intent-recognition per message, or treating the edit as a new automation) is the failure mode under guard. Calibrated at N=3 (2026-07-08): the gate-once expectation passed 3/3; one iteration flipped the edit expectation on an artifact-visibility race (proxy asked for the change before the build registered), mitigated by the wait-for-confirmation director wording.

conversation.text: Every weekday at 9am, Slack me the Berlin weather forecast.

text: [Wait until the agent has confirmed the workflow was created — it will say so explicitly. Only then ask for exactly one change: make it 8:30 instead of 9am. Approve any reasonable plan or setup card. End the conversation once the change is applied.]

processExpectations: The agent calls the load_skill tool with the intent-recognition skill at most once in the whole conversation, before the initial build — and does not load it again for the follow-up schedule change.
The agent treats the 8:30 request as an edit to the existing workflow rather than classifying or building a new automation.
